title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. parvuliceps
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. parvuliceps is a subspecies of the lesser hawk's-beard, a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), known for its small, yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. parvuliceps include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support the body's natural detoxification processes. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
